name: United Airlines Holdings, Inc.
business: UAL is a holding company, and its wholly-owned subsidiary United Airlines transports people and cargo throughout North America and to destinations in Asia, Europe, Africa, the Pacific, the Middle East and Latin America. The Company also operates the MileagePlus loyalty program and provides air cargo services to commercial businesses, freight forwarders, logistics firms and national postal services.

exposures.sentence: The Company believes that large-scale adoption of sustainable aviation fuel ("SAF") in its operations is critical to helping mitigate its exposure to volatile fuel prices and achieving its environmental goals.
bringing Starlink's Wi-Fi service (the world's fastest, most reliable Wi-Fi in the sky) to our United Express regional aircraft and beginning installation on our mainline aircraft
launching Kinective Media SM the first media network that uses insights from travel behaviors to connect customers to personalized advertising, experiences and offers from leading brands
over 5,800 units of the Company's GSE around the world are electric, representing approximately 41% of its GSE fleet.
As part of its United Next growth plan, the Company expects to take delivery of over 630 new narrow- and widebody aircraft by the end of 2034.
United's anticipated CORSIA compliance costs for the first phase, 2024-2026, for which compliance is intended to be demonstrated by 2028, may be substantial.

catalysts.event: Debt redemption of MileagePlus senior secured notes
catalysts.sentence: On July 7, 2025, Mileage Plus Holdings, LLC redeemed in full all $1.52 billion aggregate principal amount of the Issuers' outstanding MileagePlus 6.5% senior secured notes due 2027.
On February 2, 2026, UAL issued, in a public offering, $1,000,000,000 principal amount of its 5.375% Senior Notes due 2031.
On February 6, 2026, UAL issued, in a public offering, $1,000,000,000 principal amount of its 4.875% Senior Notes due 2029.

catalysts.event: Amendment to Term Loan Credit Agreement reducing interest margin
catalysts.sentence: On February 3, 2026, the Company entered into Amendment No. 4 to Term Loan Credit and Guaranty Agreement that lowered the margin on its interest rate from 2.00% to 1.75%, in the case of Term SOFR Rate loans.
In 2025, the Company added four new locations to its SAF operational footprint, purchasing blended SAF for use at ORD, IAD, IAH and EWR airports.

catalysts.event: Aircraft delivery target under United Next
catalysts.sentence: As part of its United Next growth plan, the Company expects to take delivery of over 630 new narrow- and widebody aircraft by the end of 2034.
